title: Admin inbox — every session in one scannable queue
problem: Marc needs a single page that lists every session (every status, every visitor) sorted by what needs attention first. AdminInbox is that. Reads /api/sessions (admin scope), groups by status, sorts within groups by SLA pressure (oldest triage at the top), and surfaces enough metadata per row to triage without opening each session. Clicking a row navigates to /admin/inbox/:id which is the SessionPage in admin mode.
analysis: GET /api/sessions on mount; admin scope returns every non-deleted session. Group client-side by status (triage, active, etc.), sort within each group by SLA pressure (computeSla from src/lib/format.ts — hours since creation vs. the 72h SLA constant). Each row shows: visitor email + first-name, problem type label, status pill, time since creation, SLA badge (green/amber/red). Click → navigate to /admin/inbox/:id which reuses SessionPage in operator mode.

decisions.decision: Reuse /api/sessions for inbox view, not a dedicated /api/admin/inbox endpoint
decisions.rationale: The list endpoint already returns admin-scope data when called with admin cookie. Adding a parallel endpoint would duplicate auth gating and projection logic. The /me dashboard and /admin/inbox call the same endpoint with different rendering — SQL is the projection.
decisions.recommendation: When adding a new admin-facing view of sessions, render from the same endpoint. If a new view needs fundamentally different shape, evaluate a new endpoint, not the inbox shortcut.

decisions.decision: SLA pressure sort within status groups, not status-then-time
decisions.rationale: An operator scanning the inbox should see the most-urgent triage at the top — a 70h-old triage is more important than a 5h-old triage. Status grouping keeps similar work together; SLA pressure within ranks them. Pure time-sort would mix freshly-created and aged across statuses.
decisions.recommendation: Don't reorder. The grouping is the affordance.

successCriteria:
- Lists every non-deleted session in admin scope
- Groups by status with most-urgent first within each group
- SLA badge color reflects pressure (green / amber / red)
- Click navigates to /admin/inbox/:id which is SessionPage in operator mode
- Single fetch on mount; no per-row request
implementation: src/pages/AdminInbox.tsx — useEffect → listSessions. Sorted via computeSla + status-keyed bucketing. Reuses problem-type labels from src/lib/intakeSchemas.ts.
userGuide: Land here from /admin sidebar or the hub. Each row links to /admin/inbox/:id which renders SessionPage in operator mode (full visitor + admin controls). To filter by status or search visitor email: not implemented today; use Ctrl+F. To export the inbox: not implemented; SELECT directly in D1.
knownLimitations: No filter UI (status, problem type, email search) — single-operator scale, Ctrl+F is enough
No bulk operations — every status flip is per-session via the session page
Sort is client-side; a very large inbox (~1000+ rows) would be slow. Current scale: <100

statusHistory.reason: Page shipped with the grouped + SLA-sorted inbox view.
Code-first doc co-located. Reuse-/api/sessions pattern stable.
